string.text:
The Mannlicher M95 rifle, originally designed as a straight-pull bolt-action rifle for the Austro-Hungarian army, was known for its fast cycling mechanism and distinctive en-bloc clip system. When chambered in the powerful 7.62x54r, this rifle takes on a more formidable role, combining the robust, reliable design of the M95 with the hard-hitting, long-range capabilities of the Russian cartridge. This combination offers a unique blend of rapid fire and stopping power, making it a potent choice for both military and sporting applications, particularly in environments where durability and accuracy are paramount.
 
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: ZF-4 Scope
Steel clip for the Mannlicher 95, holds 5 rounds of 8x56r Mannlicher ammunition. \n \n

The 8×56mmR Mannlicher is a rimmed rifle cartridge developed for Austro-Hungarian and later Austrian military service, known for its use in the Steyr M95 series rifles and distinctive 208-grain spitzer bullet. 
 
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Highest pierced armour class: %c[ui_gray_1]I 
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Tissue damage rating: %c[ui_gray_1]very high
A standard-issue pack of 8×56mmR Mannlicher ammunition contained two stripper clips, each loaded with five rimmed cartridges, totaling 10 rounds, ready for use with Steyr M95 rifles. \n \n
